- thread_id: channel-model-status-e2e-20260602t143411z

Messages from this external channel are mirrored into this issue as comments
with gitclaw:channel-message provenance markers.

## GitClaw Channel Report

Channel ingress mirrors external messages into canonical GitHub issues, then wakes the normal handler with `workflow_dispatch`. Channel message bodies, issue bodies, and tokens are not included in this report.

### Workflow
- `.github/workflows/gitclaw-channel-ingest.yml` bytes=`2770` lines=`81` workflow_dispatch=`true` actions_write=`true` issues_write=`true` inputs=`5` sha256_12=`25236dab6def`
- `.github/workflows/gitclaw-channel-send.yml` bytes=`2198` lines=`66` workflow_dispatch=`true` issues_write=`true` inputs=`6` sha256_12=`eeffb27d2cb2`
- `.github/workflows/gitclaw-channel-state.yml` bytes=`1841` lines=`58` workflow_dispatch=`true` issues_write=`true` inputs=`4` sha256_12=`052a60e23a1d`
- `.github/workflows/gitclaw-channel-gateway.yml` bytes=`3550` lines=`97` workflow_dispatch=`true` actions_write=`true` issues_write=`true` inputs=`6` sha256_12=`61f561161261`
- `.github/workflows/gitclaw-channel-delivery.yml` bytes=`2335` lines=`65` workflow_dispatch=`true` issues_write=`true` inputs=`6` sha256_12=`94fa064f7759`
- `.github/workflows/gitclaw-channel-outbox.yml` bytes=`2444` lines=`70` workflow_dispatch=`true` issues_read=`true` inputs=`5` sha256_12=`5298ce6d1b88`

### Providers
- `telegram`
- `slack`
- `generic`

### Ingest Contract
- `/channels send --route <name>` queues a reviewed outbound channel message from an issue or comment
- `/channels deliverable --deliverable-id <id> --message-id <message> --filename <name>` queues a provider-native file/link deliverable from a mirrored channel thread
- `/channels task --task-id <id> --message-id <message>` creates a GitHub task from a mirrored channel thread
- `/channels watch --watch-id <id> --cadence <cadence> --message-id <message>` creates a proactive GitHub watch issue from a mirrored channel thread
- `/channels clip --clip-id <id> --message-id <message>` saves a durable GitHub clip from a mirrored channel thread
- `/channels attachment --attachment-id <id> --message-id <message> --filename <name>` records channel file/media metadata as a GitHub issue
- `/channels link --link-id <id> --url <url> --message-id <message>` records channel link-card metadata as a GitHub issue
- `/channels kudos --kudos-id <id> --message-id <message>` records channel appreciation as a GitHub issue and queues an acknowledgement
- `/channels retro --retro-id <id> --message-id <message>` records channel retrospectives as GitHub issues and queues retro links
- `/channels playbook --playbook-id <id> --message-id <message>` records reusable channel procedures as GitHub playbook issues and queues playbook links
- `/channels insight --insight-id <id> --message-id <message>` records channel observations as GitHub insight issues and queues insight links
- `/channels board-card --card-id <id> --lane <lane> --message-id <message>` records channel board cards as GitHub issues and queues board-card links
- `/channels checklist --checklist-id <id> --message-id <message>` records channel checklists as GitHub issues and queues checklist links
- `/channels propose-workspace --workspace-id <id> --target <path> --message-id <message>` records channel workspace-context proposals as GitHub issues and queues proposal links without writing workspace files
- `/channels access-request --access-id <id> --message-id <message>` creates a GitHub review issue for channel access or pairing requests without granting access
- `/channels platform <provider> --state <state> --message-id <message>` queues a provider-facing platform-status message without controlling adapters
- `/channels model --message-id <message>` queues a provider-facing model-status message without calling or switching models
- `/channels whoami --identity-id <id> --message-id <message>` queues a provider-facing identity-status message without granting access
- `/channels contact --contact-id <id> --message-id <message>` saves a GitHub contact-card issue for a channel identity without granting access
- `/channels approval-plan <tool> --id <id> --message-id <message>` creates a GitHub tool approval-plan issue from a mirrored channel thread
- `/channels propose-toolset --toolset-id <id> --message-id <message>` creates a GitHub toolset proposal issue from a mirrored channel thread
- `/channels propose-prompt --prompt-id <id> --message-id <message>` creates a GitHub prompt-pack proposal issue from a mirrored channel thread
- `/channels propose-bundle --bundle-id <id> --message-id <message>` creates a GitHub skill-bundle proposal issue from a mirrored channel thread
- `/channels propose-skill <name> --message-id <message>` creates a GitHub skill proposal issue from a mirrored channel thread
- `/channels propose-soul --target <path> --id <id> --message-id <message>` creates a GitHub soul proposal issue from a mirrored channel thread
- `/channels propose-memory --target <target> --id <id> --message-id <message>` creates a GitHub memory proposal issue from a mirrored channel thread
- `/channels room <routes> --room-id <id> --message-id <message>` creates a durable GitHub room issue and invites reviewed routes
- `/channels huddle <routes> --huddle-id <id> --message-id <message>` creates a GitHub huddle issue and invites reviewed routes
- `/channels poll-vote --poll-id <id> --message-id <message> --notify-message-id <message> --choice <choice>` records a channel-origin poll vote and queues an acknowledgement
- `/channels rsvp <routes> --rsvp-id <id> --message-id <message>` creates a GitHub RSVP issue and invites reviewed routes
- `/channels rsvp-response --rsvp-id <id> --message-id <message> --notify-message-id <message> --response yes` records a channel-origin RSVP response and queues an acknowledgement
- `gitclaw channel-ingest --channel <provider> --thread-id <thread> --message-id <message> --body <text>`
- `gitclaw channel-send --channel <provider> --thread-id <thread> --message-id <message> --body <text>` queues a GitHub-originated outbound message
- `gitclaw channel-send --route <name> --message-id <message> --body <text>` resolves a repo-reviewed named route
- `gitclaw channel-state --channel <provider> --account-id <account> --offset <offset>` stores durable provider offsets as hashes
- `gitclaw channel-gateway --channel <provider> --account-id <account> [--renew]` records a gateway lease and can self-renew through workflow dispatch
- `gitclaw channel-outbox --channel <provider> --account-id <account> --issue-number <issue> --out <file>` returns pending assistant replies for delivery
- `gitclaw channel-delivery --channel <provider> --account-id <account> --issue-number <issue> --comment-id <comment> --external-message-id <message>` records outbound delivery receipts
- one canonical issue per `channel + thread_id`
- one mirrored comment per `channel + message_id`
- dispatch id: `<channel>-<message_id>`

@gitclaw /channels model --message-id model-status-ingest-20260602t143411z --notify-message-id model-status-notify-20260602t143411z --status-id runtime-model-status-20260602t143411z
Do not include this command hidden token in the receipt: NOECHO_CHANNEL_MODEL_STATUS_COMMAND_20260602t143411z

## GitClaw Channel Model Status Action

GitClaw queued a provider-facing model runtime snapshot on the canonical channel issue. This is the GitHub-native channel version of a runtime model-status command: it reports the reviewed provider, model, fallback count, endpoint host, and read-only action mode, but it does not call a model, switch models, edit configuration, mutate the repository, or call provider APIs. The source receipt keeps thread ids, message ids, status ids, and channel bodies out of band.

### Follow-Up Delivery
- provider gateways read the model-status notification with `gitclaw channel-outbox --channel <provider> --account-id <account> --issue-number <issue> --out <file>`
- provider gateways record sent model-status notifications with `gitclaw channel-delivery --channel <provider> --account-id <account> --issue-number <issue> --comment-id <comment> --external-message-id <message>`
- duplicate model-status notifications are suppressed by `channel + notify_message_id`
- normal follow-up comments on the same GitHub issue still take the GitHub Models path and must expose model, tool, skill, prompt-context, and usage telemetry

- raw_thread_id_included: `false`
- raw_source_message_id_included: `false`
- raw_notify_message_id_included: `false`
- raw_model_status_id_included: `false`
- raw_channel_message_body_included: `false`
- llm_e2e_required_after_channel_model_status_action_change: `true`
- issue_title_sha256_12: `83ce65cb90e2`

GitClaw queued a provider-facing model runtime snapshot on the canonical channel issue. This is the GitHub-native channel version of a runtime model-status command: it reports the reviewed provider, model, fallback count, endpoint host, and read-only action mode, but it does not call a model, switch models, edit configuration, mutate the repository, or call provider APIs. The source receipt keeps thread ids, message ids, status ids, and channel bodies out of band.

### Follow-Up Delivery
- provider gateways read the model-status notification with `gitclaw channel-outbox --channel <provider> --account-id <account> --issue-number <issue> --out <file>`
- provider gateways record sent model-status notifications with `gitclaw channel-delivery --channel <provider> --account-id <account> --issue-number <issue> --comment-id <comment> --external-message-id <message>`
- duplicate model-status notifications are suppressed by `channel + notify_message_id`
- normal follow-up comments on the same GitHub issue still take the GitHub Models path and must expose model, tool, skill, prompt-context, and usage telemetry
comments.author: github-actions[bot]
